title: How the Human Body Digests Dietary Protein
summary: Completed deep research on human dietary protein digestion covering gastric, pancreatic, and brush-border phases; amino acid and peptide absorption; and comparative digestion kinetics of whey (fast), casein (slow), and plant proteins (variable). Deliverables: text answer, expanded report (MD/DOCX), mind map PNG, 9-slide deck with embedded mind map, and 4 downloaded PDFs. Six peer-reviewed/government sources cited; success criteria met.
assumptions: Adult human physiology in healthy individuals with intact digestive function
Mixed-meal context unless comparing isolated protein supplements
Bovine dairy whey and casein as reference for fast vs slow animal proteins
Plant protein comparison uses common supplemental sources (soy, pea, rice, blends) rather than every whole-food matrix
Digestibility figures refer to processed isolates/concentrates where noted; whole-food plant matrices may digest more slowly
Deep report expanded in markdown after initial create_research_report call due to JSON payload limits in the tool
